bear_case: We have talked ourselves into treating an absence of evidence as a neutral fact because we have an elegant mechanism for handling it. The Proof Protocol is not neutral - it is a way of manufacturing a reason to stay engaged with a file that contains nothing. A solo founder with five years of unverifiable NDA work and four unfalsifiable-from-outside claims is, on the base rate, someone whose numbers came from a spreadsheet rather than a benchmark. The challenge we designed is one they can pass by being a competent engineer for a weekend, which is a much lower bar than the one the deck implies they have already cleared.
weakest_evidence: Every number in the file originates from the founder. There is not one independent observation of any kind.
The NDA explanation for zero public work is accepted, not verified. We cannot verify it and we have not tried.
The market axis scores 0.7 on confidence 0.44, derived from two deck slides. That number is closer to a guess than we are comfortable admitting on a scorecard.
Entity resolution returned NEW with no aliases. We do not actually know that no public work exists - only that we did not find any.
load_bearing_claim: recall@10 >= 0.94 while reading <= 0.3% of index bytes per query, at a p99 under 900us. If recall and latency do not hold on the SAME benchmark run, there is no product - just two separately achievable numbers presented together.
spread_note: Two axes have zero spread because they have zero evidence. A zero spread here means unknown, not agreed.
what_would_change_my_mind: A challenge artifact where recall and p99 are reported from one run, with raw latency samples rather than a summary statistic.
The founder pushing back on the page-cache warming we requested. Compliance on that point would tell us the deck numbers were produced the same way.
Any independent trace of five years of storage work - a patent, a conference attendance, a former colleague willing to be named.
